title: hostapi.load_host() silently swallows a broken _host.py and reverts to Claude defaults with zero breadcrumb
evidence: load_host() does: `try: ... spec.loader.exec_module(mod); return mod.HOST\n except Exception: # noqa: BLE001 — no/broken _host.py -> Claude defaults\n    return Host()` (hostapi.py:184-191). This is the ONLY place `_host.py` is loaded, and it is a bare `except Exception` with no call to warn()/_log_gate_event or any stderr/log write before returning the fallback.
impact: On the Codex install, a syntax error, import failure, or any exception while executing plugins/ca-codex/hooks/_host.py (e.g. a partial/corrupted vendored copy, a Python version incompatibility in the file) causes EVERY entry script to silently run as if it were plain Claude Code: has_statusline reverts to True (Codex has no statusline surface), has_read_tool reverts to True, and — most consequentially — iter_file_ops falls back to the Claude Write/Edit shape instead of parsing Codex's apply_patch envelope, so tool_input['command'] (the patch text) is never decomposed into file ops at all. This is a guard-bypass-adjacent condition (a write op that should be guarded is invisible to pre-write's per-op checks), and nothing signals it happened — 'CodexHost silently loaded' and 'CodexHost failed to load, reverted to Claude defaults' are observationally identical from every artifact a maintainer can inspect (stdout, stderr, gate-events.log).
recommendation: On the except branch, emit a loud breadcrumb before falling back (e.g. warn() to stderr, or a dedicated one-line stderr print naming the exception) so a broken _host.py degrades LOUD, not silent, matching this module's own file-header discipline ('fail-loud' is called out elsewhere in _hooklib.py as the module's default posture, with _log_gate_event's own fail-open documented as 'the ONE deliberate exception').
acceptance_criteria: A _host.py that raises on import (syntax error or exception in module body) produces a visible stderr line identifying the failure before load_host() returns the Host() fallback.
A test exercises the except branch and asserts the breadcrumb is emitted (test_codex_adapter.py or hostapi's own test coverage has no such case today per grep of test_codex_adapter.py).
